results.name: Procter & Gamble
results.key_finding: Announced 7,000 layoffs (~15% of non-manufacturing workforce) despite increasing profits, crossing the Exemplary-to-Established boundary on Boundaries and Integrity.

results.name: Oracle Corporation
results.key_finding: Finalizing 30,000 layoffs with a 'sign release or forfeit severance' ultimatum and WARN notice counted into severance, dropping Developing to Critical.

results.name: Bolivia
results.key_finding: Sixth week of the worst economic crisis in four decades; ministers resigned and the government refused to rule out emergency powers and military deployment against protesters.

results.name: Mali
results.key_finding: 64% increase in famine-level conditions and 1.5M severely food insecure amid junta rule; confirms a deeply Critical profile below the 5-point threshold.

results.name: Niger
results.key_finding: 548,000 internally displaced as part of the 24.3M Sahel humanitarian need; confirms Critical profile.

results.name: Chad
results.key_finding: Part of the 24.3M Sahel appeal with large refugee-hosting strain during the June-August lean season; confirms Critical.

results.name: Burkina Faso
results.key_finding: Highest Sahel displacement (~2M IDPs) plus documented GBV and violence against children; confirms one of the lowest scores in the index.

results.name: Nigeria
results.key_finding: NE Nigeria included in the regional Sahel appeal, but evidence is region-wide and not Nigeria-disaggregated; insufficient for a band-crossing downgrade.

results.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
results.key_finding: WHO 'catastrophic collision of disease and conflict' — Bundibugyo Ebola in Ituri with no vaccine plus ~10M facing acute hunger; confirms near-floor score.

results.name: United States
results.key_finding: Record ICE detention death rate, a June 4 memo to stop reporting released-detainee deaths, and warehouse-detention expansion confirm and slightly deepen the Critical immigration-enforcement profile.

results.name: Turkey
results.key_finding: HRW 2026 documents an 'unprecedented onslaught' on the opposition, 11th consecutive Pride ban, and detained lawyers/journalists; confirms Critical.

results.name: Israel
results.key_finding: OCHA records 856 fatalities since the Oct 2025 ceasefire plus persistent aid-entry constraints; confirms the existing floor/harm-flag designation.

results.name: Palestine
results.key_finding: Governance capacity overwhelmed by blockade and destruction; aid-access constraints marginally lower Action/Accountability but the Developing profile holds.

results.name: Iran
results.key_finding: Amnesty documents 6,000+ arrests and at least 39 political executions in 2026; intensifying repression confirms the near-floor score.

results.name: Amazon
results.key_finding: EFF reports Amazon 'completely ignored' Project Nimbus letters and a Senate report found 30% above-average warehouse injuries; confirms Critical.

results.name: Weyerhaeuser
results.key_finding: No new adverse events; strong safety culture (incident rate <2 for a decade) and net-zero-by-2040 roadmap confirm the published 39.1 exactly.

results.name: Harley-Davidson
results.key_finding: DEI rollback under activist pressure and a 2025 reduction-in-force modestly lower Equity/Integrity, but strong retention keeps it Developing below the threshold.

results.name: Phillips 66
results.key_finding: No material new compassion-relevant events; refining-sector baseline profile confirmed at 25.0.

results.name: NiSource
results.key_finding: Coal-to-clean transition planning modestly lifts Systemic Thinking; overall regulated-utility profile holds at Developing.

results.name: Celanese
results.key_finding: No material new compassion-relevant events; chemical-sector baseline profile confirmed at 25.0.

errors.detail: The scan's top_entities listed 'niger' but the attached entity_review summary/sources described Nigeria. Both Niger and Nigeria were assessed as distinct entities against their correct published index entries (Niger #162, Nigeria #133).
